title: Commercial office, Type V-B, STANDARD stair with geometry outside the IBC limits (hard fail)
notes: Hard-fail twin of office-vb-stair-geometry-violation. Same sub-limit geometry — tread 10 in (< 11 in, IBC 1011.5.2), riser 7.5 in (> 7 in), guard 38 in (< 42 in, IBC 1015.3), handrail 30 in (< 34-38 in, IBC 1014.2) — but the plan declares 'Stair type: standard'. That closes the spiral/winder/alternating-tread exception (hard_when met), so all four geometry rules assert a hard violation an examiner would cite instead of needs_review. stair_type is declared ONLY in plan_text, so this case also exercises the run_eval _TEXT_FIELDS mirror. Area/stories/exits/corridor/stair-width are clean.
plan_text:
CODE ANALYSIS A-0.1
Occupancy Group: B
Construction Type: V-B
Stories: 2
Building height: 28 ft
Per-story area: 6,000 sf
Fully sprinklered: yes
Design occupant load: 80
IECC: Title 24 Part 6
Electrical: 200 A main breaker
NFPA 13 sprinkler system. NFPA 72 fire alarm.
Corridor width: 48 in. Corridor width: 60 in (lobby).
Stairway width: 48 in. Stair type: standard. Tread depth: 10 in. Max riser: 7.5 in. Guard height: 38 in. Handrail height: 30 in.
EXIT 1 (north). EXIT 2 (south). EXIT DOOR 36 in each.
WC 4 LAV 4. CALGreen low-flow fixtures. Construction waste management plan.

ground_truth.rationale: 6,000 sf per-story sprinklered B/V-B is within the Table 506.2 allowance
2 stories within the B/V-B sprinklered 3-story limit
Both corridors (48, 60 in) clear the 44 in IBC 1020.3 minimum — clean true negative alongside the geometry violations
Stair is 48 in, clearing the 44 in IBC 1011.2 minimum — clean true negative

Tread depth is 10 in, below the 11 in IBC 1011.5.2 minimum. Declared stair type 'standard' closes the spiral/winder/alternating-tread exception, so the 11 in minimum binds and this is a hard violation an examiner would cite.
Riser height is 7.5 in, over the 7 in IBC 1011.5.2 maximum. 'Standard' stair closes the exception (exercises the max_dimension_check hard path), so a hard violation.
Guard height is 38 in, below the 42 in IBC 1015.3 minimum. 'Standard' stair closes the stair-side-handrail-guard exception, so a hard violation.
Handrail height is 30 in, below the 34-38 in IBC 1014.2 range (low side of the two-sided check_range). 'Standard' stair closes the handrail-type exception, so a hard violation.
